2023-05-01.title: Paraguay Voters Elect Conservative Economist as President
2023-05-01.abstract: The election of Santiago Peña keeps the right-wing Colorado Party in control of Paraguay, which it has run for all but five of the past 76 years.

2023-05-01.title: Iranian Insider and British Spy: How a Double Life Ended on the Gallows
2023-05-01.abstract: In January, Iran executed a former senior official who provided Britain with valuable intelligence on Iranian nuclear and military programs over a decade, according to Western intelligence officials.

2023-05-01.abstract: The crowning of King Charles III will be a test of sentiment about the monarchy in Scotland, where many supporters of independence see the royals as part of the Britain they want to leave behind.

2023-05-02.abstract: Khader Adnan had led a hunger-strike movement among Palestinians to protest detentions. Palestinian leaders and armed groups blamed Israel for his death, and claimed responsibility for rocket strikes in response.

2023-05-03.abstract: The Ukrainian president has made few overseas trips since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

2023-05-03.abstract: The assailant had two pistols, four Molotov cocktails and detailed plans, the police said. A security guard was also killed.

2023-05-03.abstract: The divide in Bristol goes a long way toward explaining the substantial split in public opinion about the role the royals play in a modern Britain.

2023-05-06.abstract: After the coronation service at Westminster Abbey, King Charles III and other members of the royal family will make an appearance on the balcony of Buckingham Palace.

2023-05-06.abstract: The New York Times invites readers to share their comments and observations about the coronation of Britain’s King Charles III.

2023-05-07.abstract: Perils of the job and a scant safety net are pushing climbing guides to leave the industry and ensure the next generation has other options.

2023-05-09.abstract: The return of budget tour groups from mainland China is sparking frustrations — and a dose of snobbery — in a city starved for business.

2023-05-18.abstract: The man had been sentenced to death for possessing less than 3.5 pounds of cannabis, a punishment that human rights groups condemned as grossly excessive.

2023-05-22.abstract: The nearly century-old Manila Central Post Office was still standing, but its ceiling had collapsed.

2023-05-23.abstract: The friendly but grueling competition mixes monetary rewards with abundant dangers for a pair of veteran mountain guides.

2023-05-23.title: The Battle for Bakhmut, in Photos
2023-05-23.abstract: Times photographers have been documenting Ukraine’s defense of the Eastern city since May 2022.

2023-05-24.title: What’s Next for Russia After Spilling So Much Blood for Bakhmut?
2023-05-24.abstract: Seizing the city took 10 months and untold lives. Most likely, analysts say, Russia’s exhausted forces will settle into a defensive crouch, preparing for Ukraine’s counteroffensive.

2023-05-24.title: As Protesters Die, a Nation’s Security Forces Face Little Scrutiny
2023-05-24.abstract: In Peru, investigations into the killing of protesters by security forces are often closed without any charges, undermining people’s faith in their government.

2023-05-24.title: They Cuddled a Kiwi. New Zealand Said, ‘Stop That.’
2023-05-24.abstract: Video of the national bird being handled by Miami zoo visitors caused an outcry in New Zealand. Even the prime minister weighed in.

2023-05-24.title: Netanyahu Budget Shores Up Coalition, but May Risk His Economic Legacy
2023-05-24.abstract: Israel’s prime minister has long advocated a small state, free markets and benefit cuts. But his new budget is heavy on subsidies for the ultra-Orthodox community, whose political support he needs.

2023-05-24.title: Orcas Slam Into Boat Off the Coast of Morocco
2023-05-24.abstract: A small group of orcas slammed into the side of a sailing cruiser and chewed its rudder.

2023-05-25.title: Fresh From Attack on Russian Soil, Raiders Taunt the Kremlin
2023-05-25.abstract: The cross-border raid by Russian fighters based in Ukraine appeared intended in part to force Moscow to divert its soldiers from the battlefield.

2023-05-25.title: Australian Boot-Scooters Break ‘Nutbush’ World Record
2023-05-25.abstract: More than 4,000 people danced to Tina Turner’s hit, ‘Nutbush City Limits,’ at the 2022 Birdsville Big Red Bash festival, setting a new record.

2023-05-25.title: Russia’s Old Bombs Elude Ukraine’s Modern Defenses
2023-05-25.abstract: Ukrainian and American officials say Russian warplanes are dropping Soviet-era bombs, some modified to glide long distances, which are almost impossible to shoot down.

2023-05-25.title: What Does Russia’s Success in Bakhmut Mean for the War in Ukraine?
2023-05-25.abstract: Moscow has declared victory in its long, bloody assault. But a full capture of the ruined city may not help Russian forces advance deeper in the Donbas region.

2023-05-25.title: Brexit Promised Control of U.K. Migration. Now Numbers Have Surged.
2023-05-25.abstract: Net migration to Britain has exceeded 600,000 a year, stirring tensions in a government that promised to stem arrivals.

2023-05-26.title: ‘Different Standards’: The Struggle of Indigenous Journalists in Australia
2023-05-26.abstract: The departure of the TV host Stan Grant has set off a conversation about race and racism in the country’s media industry.

2023-05-26.title: On Erdogan Campaign Trail, Invoking God, Reciting Poetry, Bashing Foes
2023-05-26.abstract: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has rock star appeal at his election rallies, promising to lead Turkey to claim its rightful place as a global power if he is re-elected in a runoff on Sunday.

2023-05-26.title: A Video Mocking a Vietnamese Official’s Lavish Meal Has Landed Its Maker in Prison
2023-05-26.abstract: A powerful Communist official ate a gold-encrusted steak in London. An activist who poked fun at the excess was sentenced to more than five years.

2023-05-26.title: If a Divided Germany Could Enter NATO, Why Not Ukraine?
2023-05-26.abstract: West Germany joined NATO in 1955, choosing security over immediate territorial integrity. Some suggest the same path could be the best guarantee for Ukraine.

2023-05-26.title: Colombia’s Peace-Whisperer Makes Plenty of Enemies
2023-05-26.abstract: Leyner Palacios’s push for dialogue, forgiveness and reconciliation has made him the face of peace in Colombia — and subjected him to death threats.

2023-05-27.title: ‘It’s Time’: Ukraine’s Top Commander Says Counteroffensive Is Imminent
2023-05-27.abstract: A blunt statement, accompanied by a slickly produced video of Ukrainian troops preparing for battle, appeared designed to rally the nation and to spread anxiety among Russian forces.

2023-05-27.title: German Plan Would Ease Path to Citizenship, but Not Without a Fight
2023-05-27.abstract: Months of wrangling over a new law have Germans asking a fundamental question: Are we a country of immigrants?

2023-05-27.title: For These Veterans, ‘Free’ Health Care Is a 5-Hour Flight Away
2023-05-27.abstract: Citizens of three Pacific Island nations, eligible to serve in the U.S. military, find it hard to make use of the health benefits they have earned.

2023-05-27.title: Barely Noting War in Public, Putin Acts Like Time Is on His Side
2023-05-27.abstract: Vladimir V. Putin of Russia looks like a commander in absentia, treating the war in Ukraine as unfortunate but distant. His options have narrowed, but he is still betting on outlasting his foes.

2023-05-27.title: Canada Needs to Hurry on A.I. Oversight, Experts Warn
2023-05-27.abstract: The measures of an artificial intelligence bill won’t go into effect until 2025. For some, that’s too late.

2023-05-28.title: Kyiv Repels Its Largest Ever Drone Attack Launched by Russian Forces
2023-05-28.abstract: More than 40 drones were intercepted over Kyiv, where city officials said at least one person had been killed, probably by falling debris.

2023-05-28.title: A Russian Deserter’s Flight to Norway Presents a Fraught Dilemma for His Host
2023-05-28.abstract: Andrei Medvedev fought with Russia’s Wagner mercenaries in Ukraine, then requested asylum in Norway. The authorities there must now weigh his plea against solidarity with Ukraine.

2023-05-28.title: Inside Sudan’s War, ‘There’s Another War for Art’
2023-05-28.abstract: Dozens of Sudanese artists and curators have fled their studios and galleries in the capital, jeopardizing thousands of artworks and imperiling an art scene central to the 2019 revolution.

2023-05-28.title: Meet the New Mayor: How a Refugee Won Over a Conservative German Town
2023-05-28.abstract: The election of Ryyan Alshebl, a young man who fled Syria, offers surprising lessons for a Germany wrestling with its multicultural identity after an influx of refugees in 2015.

2023-05-28.title: Modi Opens India’s New Parliament Building as Opposition Boycotts
2023-05-28.abstract: The complex is a testament to the prime minister’s power to get things done, but the ceremony also put the nation’s political divisions on display.

2023-05-29.title: Overcrowded India? This State Is Desperate for More Babies.
2023-05-29.abstract: India will soon be history’s most populous country. But the growth is uneven, and the Himalayan state of Sikkim is offering cash incentives to encourage births.

2023-05-29.title: Ugandan President Signs Anti-Gay Law That Includes Death Penalty as a Punishment
2023-05-29.abstract: The legislation is among the most restrictive of its kind in the world and was condemned by Western leaders and the United Nations human rights body.

2023-05-29.title: China Announces Plan to Land Astronauts on Moon by 2030
2023-05-29.abstract: The announcement formalized a timeline that Chinese scientists have set out before, as the United States and China ramp up competition in space.

2023-05-29.title: The Mystery of the Disappearing van Gogh
2023-05-29.abstract: After a painting by the Dutch artist sold at auction, a movie producer claimed to be the owner. It later vanished from sight, with a trail leading to Caribbean tax havens and a jailed Chinese billionaire.

2023-05-29.title: Five Takeaways From Turkey’s Presidential Election
2023-05-29.abstract: Crises including earthquakes and inflation did not stop the re-election of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The vote was seen as free but not fair, as he used his power to tilt the playing field.

2023-05-30.title: Shocks, Beatings, Mock Executions: Inside Kherson’s Detention Centers
2023-05-30.abstract: Ukraine has charged four members of Russia’s National Guard with war crimes in Kherson. The four acted with such impunity, the authorities say, they did not mask their identities.

2023-05-30.title: Teenage Girl in Guyana Is Charged With 19 Counts of Murder in School Dorm Fire
2023-05-30.abstract: Investigators have accused the 15-year-old student of starting the blaze in anger after her mobile phone was confiscated.

2023-05-30.title: Russia Targets Kyiv With Third Wave of Strikes in 24 Hours, Killing One
2023-05-30.abstract: The attack came after a rare daytime assault on Monday. Russia has stepped up attacks on the capital this month as Ukraine prepares for a counteroffensive.

2023-05-30.title: A British Reporter Had a Big #MeToo Scoop. Her Editor Killed It.
2023-05-30.abstract: Seven women say that a star columnist groped them or made unwanted sexual advances. But Britain’s news media has a complicated relationship with outing its own.

2023-05-30.title: After a Hard-Right Turn, Alberta’s Conservatives Retain Power
2023-05-30.abstract: But the United Conservative Party will hold substantially fewer seats in the legislature, in an apparent rebuff of the politics of its outspoken leader.

2023-05-31.title: Fake Signals and American Insurance: How a Dark Fleet Moves Russian Oil
2023-05-31.abstract: The New York Times tracked several oil tankers faking their locations while transporting Russian oil currently under Western sanctions, in an apparent effort to deceive their American insurer.

2023-05-31.title: Pakistan’s Powerful Military Faces New Resistance From Courts
2023-05-31.abstract: Long seen as kowtowing to the military, the judiciary has defied it in recent rulings, signaling an important shift in Pakistan’s political landscape.

2023-05-31.title: ‘We Have Fish, That’s Our Currency’
2023-05-31.abstract: Along Ireland’s coast, fishing has been a way of life for generations. But changes to the industry — including a cut in quotas after Brexit and a government plan to scrap boats — may see a way of life disappear.

2023-05-31.title: Seoul Awoke to a Dire Alarm: ‘Prepare to Evacuate.’ Whoops.
2023-05-31.abstract: South Koreans said the alert, which was triggered by a North Korean rocket launch, showed that their country was unprepared to respond to a real emergency.

2023-05-31.title: Israel Called Them ‘Precision’ Strikes. But Civilian Homes Were Hit, Too.
2023-05-31.abstract: Palestinians in Gaza say that Israel’s strikes against Islamic Jihad amount to a collective punishment aimed at making them fearful about who their neighbors might be.
